Pre-existing syntax error on line 36 (.then(() => )) — empty arrow function body — caused MV3 service worker registration to fail with Chrome Status Code 15. Defect predates Strike 024 (operator confirmed via runtime load attempt). Fixed: '.then(() => )' → '.then(() => {})' (empty block expresses 'nothing to do on success'; minimal change preserving original intent). HONEST CORRECTION per CLAUDE-INTEGRITY: my Strike-024 syntax checks ran 'node --check' in script mode (CommonJS-default, lenient) which falsely passed; the operator's runtime load (or the equivalent 'node --input-type=module --check') correctly flags the SyntaxError at line 36 column 15. Validation now uses module mode going forward — matches MV3 service worker behavior. All 7 chronicle JS files re-checked in module mode: 7/7 OK. SW now load-ready.

Strike 026: Chronicle payment-flow debug + UX-feedback hardening. Operator 6-task directive: (1) debug Chronicle payment success flow; (2) ensure SW writes TIER_VAULT + broadcasts TIER_UNLOCKED; (3) ExtPay redirect-to-options.html-on-success; (4) shared lib/options-tier-init.js force tier-check on window focus; (5) 'Payment Successful!' toast on TIER_UNLOCKED; (6) log + Factory Manifest v1.8. Pre-execution diligence: read ExtPay.js for redirect support; re-read chronicle SW + extpay-wrapper.js for latent bugs.

Tasks 1+2+3. Three behavioral changes inside the onPaid() handler: (1) diagnostic logging at 4 inflection points (onPaid fired with email + paid status; setTier committed; TIER_UNLOCKED broadcast sent OR no-listener; redirect outcome); critical observability for the payment-success path — operator can verify each step in chrome://extensions DevTools. (2) Error handling: setTier wrapped in try/catch, returns early on persistence failure (do not broadcast TIER_UNLOCKED if storage write didn't land). sendMessage wrapped in try/catch (rejects when no Options tab open; not fatal — log + continue). (3) NEW best-effort 'redirect' to options.html: queries chrome.tabs for tabs matching chrome-extension://{id}/options/options.html*; if found, calls chrome.tabs.update(active: true) + chrome.windows.update(focused: true); if not found, logs (user can re-open manually). ExtPay SDK verified to have NO native redirect param (open_payment_page only takes plan_nickname); this is the code-side substitute.

Task 4. Appended window.addEventListener('focus', () => { renderTier(); refreshDevTierLabel(); }) after the existing TIER_UNLOCKED listener. Belt-and-suspenders coverage: chrome.storage.onChanged also re-renders on tier writes (chronicle-side), TIER_UNLOCKED runtime.sendMessage covers the SW-broadcast path, and now focus covers the case where the page was loaded BEFORE the storage write AND the runtime broadcast was missed (e.g., user manually switches back from Stripe checkout tab to options tab).

Task 5. Updated the existing TIER_UNLOCKED runtime.onMessage listener (Strike 024) to split UNLOCKED vs DOWNGRADED branches. UNLOCKED branch: calls renderTierUI() + shows toast('Payment Successful! Vault unlocked.', 5000ms). DOWNGRADED branch: renderTierUI() + toast('Vault locked — payment status changed.', 5000ms). Toast duration extended to 5s (default 3s) so user has time to read the confirmation. The SW already does the persistence + log; this is the visible user-facing confirmation.

Fleet sync after canonical update. 12/12 SHA-identical to canonical post-distribution. 12/12 module-mode syntax-clean per node --input-type=module --check. The other 11 extensions get the focus listener for free even though only chronicle is currently wired to ExtPay (Strike 024) — when their own ExtPay rollouts land per blueprint §VI, the focus refresh is already in place.

Task 3 refactor per operator selection (storage-passed tab ID; no new 'tabs' permission). TWO coordinated edits: (1) options.js onUnlockVault now calls chrome.tabs.getCurrent() to capture the options tab's own ID, then writes it to chrome.storage.local.paymentReturnTabId BEFORE calling openPaymentPage(). Defensive: skips storage write if getCurrent returns undefined (unusual context). (2) SW onPaid handler reads chrome.storage.local.paymentReturnTabId; if it's a number, calls chrome.tabs.update(id, {active: true}) + chrome.windows.update(windowId, {focused: true}); clears the key after one-shot use. chrome.tabs.update with known tab ID does NOT require 'tabs' permission — only chrome.tabs.query({url:...}) does, which we now avoid. No manifest change; no Chrome Web Store re-prompt. If the recorded tab was closed by the user, chrome.tabs.update throws; we catch + log + rely on Task 4 focus listener as fallback. Trade-off: slightly more code than the query approach; one less permission.

Strike 027: Chronicle payment-persistence repair + cross-fleet ExtPay fail-safe. Operator 3-task directive: (1) ensure SW onPaid listener correctly registered + persists TIER_VAULT (close the wrapper race window noted in Strike 026 diligence); (2) lib/options-tier-init.js mandatory extpay.getUser() sync on page load + window focus; (3) Factory Manifest v1.85. Building on Strike 026's 3-layer defense (broadcast + focus listener + storage.onChanged) — adding a 4th layer: ExtPay-as-source-of-truth poll-on-load + poll-on-focus.

Task 1 part A. Refactored initPayments() to accept an optional onPaidCallback parameter; registers the listener BEFORE extpay.startBackground() polls. Closes the theoretical race window noted in Strike 026 diligence: ExtPay's first poll could previously fire between startBackground() and a separately-deferred addListener() call, causing a missed payment-confirmation event on SW restart when the user had already paid in a prior session. The exported onPaid() function is retained for backwards compatibility + secondary listeners. Added JSDoc documenting both patterns (preferred single-call vs legacy).

Task 1 part B. Switched SW from the two-call pattern (initPayments(); onPaid(handler);) to the single-call pattern (initPayments(handler);). Import of onPaid removed; only initPayments needed now. Updated header comment to document the Strike-027 race-closure rationale. Closing comment at end of handler block notes 'end of initPayments(onPaidCallback)' for readers tracing the bracket-matching.

Task 2. Added async function tryExtPaySync() that dynamic-imports ./payments/extpay-wrapper.js (try/catch — graceful no-op for the 11 extensions without a wrapper); reads mod.getCurrentTier(); if remote='vault' and local!='vault', calls setTier(TIER_VAULT) + direct renderTier()+refreshDevTierLabel() re-render. Upgrade-only (downgrade for refund handling is its own concern per blueprint §V FM4). Wired on init (after renderTier/initDevOverride/injectBrandMission) AND inside the existing window.focus listener (alongside renderTier+refreshDevTierLabel). 4th layer in the defense-in-depth payment-confirmation chain (joins runtime.sendMessage TIER_UNLOCKED + focus listener + chronicle's storage.onChanged).

The 11 extensions without an extpay-wrapper.js will have tryExtPaySync() silently no-op via the dynamic-import catch (no errors thrown; no log spam). When their own ExtPay wrappers land per blueprint §VI generalization, the fail-safe activates automatically.
